title: loasaceae
intro: The Loasaceae rank refers to a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the loasa or wolf's-claw family. This family consists of approximately 85 species spread across 6-8 genera, primarily found in the Americas. The Loasaceae family is characterized by their unique flowers, often with a distinctive spur or claw, and their ability to thrive in a wide range of environments. Members of this family are often used in medicine and have been a subject of interest in plant research due to their unique properties and adaptations.
